title: Roldana acutangula
taxonomy: Roldana acutangula is classified within the family Asteraceae. This classification places it among the daisy or sunflower family, which consists of a wide range of flowering plants. At the genus level, Roldana acutangula is part of the Roldana genus, which includes various species of shrubs and perennials. The species is native to the mountainous regions of Mexico and Central America, where it thrives in well-drained soil and full sun. As a member of the Asteraceae family, Roldana acutangula exhibits characteristics typical of this family, such as composite flowers and a taproot system.
uses: Roldana acutangula has been utilized in traditional medicine for its purported benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. intro: Roldana acutangula, also known as Mexican Gold, is a perennial plant belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as fever, sore throats, and respiratory issues. Roldana acutangula contains bioactive constituents like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Roldana acutangula is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and salves, due to its purported antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects.
